stories.headline: US House passes War Powers Resolution to end Trump's war in Iran

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: The Iran War Powers Resolution that I cosponsored (opposing the war) just passed the House of Representatives. The People’s House is sending a message: end this war.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: BREAKING: House votes 215-208 to end President Trump's war in Iran.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Trump’s illegal war in Iran has descended our world and our global economy into chaos. Yesterday, the House finally met the threshold to pass a War Powers Resolution. The Senate must work quickly to advance this resolution and end this war.

stories.headline: SAVE America Act fails in Senate after four GOP senators join Democrats

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Unbelievable. 19 Republicans just voted with all Democrats to send more money to Ukraine…

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: 🔥🚨 MAJOR JUST IN: The SAVE America Act Just Failed In The Senate. The measure, which would have required voter ID and proof of citizenship nationwide, was rejected 48-50.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: More than a dozen GOP lawmakers defied their own leadership — and President Donald Trump — by voting with Democrats to approve a major bill to deliver billions of dollars in aid to Ukraine

stories.body: Fidelity has lowered the minimum account requirement for access to the SpaceX IPO from as much as $500,000 to just $2,000.

stories.headline: Capital markets fund AI buildout amid Bitcoin ETF outflows.
stories.body: Capital markets are funding the AI buildout at historic scale: ~$400B over 6 months. Bitcoin ETFs have seen ~$4B of outflows since May 14, pressuring $BTC. This is a capital rotation, not a Bitcoin impairment. Volatility creates opportunity.

stories.headline: NBA player Terry Rozier must forfeit $26M salary over gambling scheme
stories.body: NBA free agent Terry Rozier must forfeit most of his $26.6 million salary for the 2025-2026 season due to his alleged role in a sports gambling scheme, an arbitrator rules.

stories.headline: Scientists warn over White House proposal to control research funding
stories.body: Scientists across multiple disciplines are sounding the alarm after the White House proposed taking greater control over how scientific research gets funded and allowing political appointees to decide whether to approve scientific grants. https://www.cnn.com/2026/06/04/climate/trump-omb-funding-rule-climate-science?Date=20260604&Profile=CNN&utm_content=1780599522&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter

stories.headline: Anthropic urges global pause in AI development over self-improvement risks
stories.body: Anthropic is calling for top AI labs to weigh slowing the pace of development, suggesting that AI systems are advancing so rapidly that they may soon be able to improve themselves without human intervention in ways that could pose societal risks. https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-urges-global-pause-in-ai-development-flags-self-improvement-risk-99cefb73?mod=e2tw

stories.headline: NVIDIA announces shipping Nemotron 3 Ultra, a 550B MoE frontier-intelligence open model.
stories.body:
Today we're shipping Nemotron 3 Ultra.

A 550B MoE frontier-intelligence open model built for long-running agents.

It delivers 5x faster inference and lowers the cost of complex agentic tasks by up to 30% versus other open frontier models.

stories.headline: California tech executive in $35M Newport Beach mansion arrested for supplying US tech to Iran
stories.body:
THE ENEMY IS HERE: California tech executive and dual U.S.-Iranian citizen arrested for allegedly running a decades-long scheme to illegally sell critical U.S. computer equipment to Iran’s military and nuclear programs.

The wealthy Orange County businessman, who lived in a $35 million mansion in Newport Beach, is accused of selling components directly to Iran’s Atomic Energy Organization between 2017 and 2023, in violation of U.S. sanctions.

U.S. Attorney: “Aiding our declared enemy… We will hold him accountable by seeking an appropriate prison sentence and seizing his assets, including the $35 million mansion.”

Authorities say this highlights the need for stronger vetting and enforcement of sanctions against those helping adversarial regimes. LOCK HIM UP AND TOSS THE KEYS.
